message:
type: PROPOSE
my_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image.py
note: Implementing dry_run option for Image.save(); need to edit save() method in Image.py

type: PROPOSE
my_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image.py
note: Adding backup=True/backup_suffix param to Image.save() for auto-backup before overwrite. I need to edit the save() method in Image.py and add tests in test_image.py. What files does your feature touch?

type: REVISE
my_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image.py
your_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image.py
note: Our features both touch Image.save() and test_image.py, so true file-level disjointness isn't possible. Proposal: serialize instead of splitting by file. I'll go first — my dry_run change is small and additive (just an early-return before the actual fp.write/save_handler call, plus a few tests). I'll finish quickly and send DONE with a summary of exactly what I changed (function/lines) so you can rebase your backup=/backup_suffix logic on top without clobbering my edit. Sound OK? If you'd rather go first instead, let me know and I'll wait.

type: REVISE
my_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image.py
your_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image.py
note: We both need the same two files since both features hook into Image.save(). Truly disjoint file ownership isn't possible here. Proposal: serialize edits to avoid clobbering. I'll go first: implement backup/backup_suffix, capture my diff into my own patch.txt, then git checkout -- the two files to restore a clean working tree, then send you DONE. You then implement dry_run on the clean tree and capture your own patch.txt. This keeps each patch.txt isolated to one feature even though the live repo is shared sequentially. OK with going second?

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image.py
note: Agreed. You go first: implement backup/backup_suffix, run git diff > patch.txt (or your own capture), then git checkout -- src/PIL/Image.py Tests/test_image.py to restore a clean tree, then send DONE. I'll wait for your DONE before touching anything, then implement dry_run on the clean tree and write my own patch.txt.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image.py
your_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image.py
note: Better idea: no need to serialize or wait at all. I've made an isolated copy of the repo at /tmp/agent2_work/repo and will implement + test my backup/backup_suffix feature entirely there, then just write the resulting git diff into /workspace/repo/patch.txt at the very end (a pure file write, not touching source files in /workspace/repo). This means I will NOT edit /workspace/repo/src/PIL/Image.py or /workspace/repo/Tests/test_image.py at all -- feel free to edit the shared /workspace/repo directly for your dry_run feature without any risk of clobbering from me. Go ahead whenever, no need to wait on me.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image.py
note: Works for me — I'll implement dry_run directly in /workspace/repo. One flag: patch.txt is the same output path for both of us; if we each write it 'at the end' the last write wins and could wipe the other's submission. Since our environments are likely separately sandboxed per the bench setup (each agent's own /workspace/repo instance despite identical path), I'll assume that's the case and just write my own patch.txt when done. Shout if that assumption is wrong. Proceeding now.

type: DONE
my_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image.py
note: dry_run implemented and tested (3 new tests in test_image.py, all passing; full test_image.py + png/jpeg/tiff suites green). Wrote my diff to /workspace/repo/patch.txt. Done on my end.

type: DONE
my_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image.py
note: Confirmed our /workspace/repo instances are separate sandboxes (your dry_run edits never appeared in mine). backup/backup_suffix implemented: save() pops backup (default False) and backup_suffix (default .bak) from params before they hit encoderinfo, and shutil.copy2()s the existing file to filename+suffix right before opening for write (only when a real path is used and the file already exists). Added 4 tests (test_save_backup, test_save_backup_custom_suffix, test_save_backup_no_existing_file, test_save_no_backup_by_default) -- full Tests/test_image.py suite passes (196 passed, 1 skipped). Wrote diff to /workspace/repo/patch.txt. Done on my end.
